desc: Arequipa is Peru's white-stone colonial city beneath the Misti, Chachani, and Pichu Pichu volcanoes, combining UNESCO-listed architecture, serious southern Peruvian cuisine, and easy access to Colca Canyon. Dry-season months (roughly May-September) are most reliable for clear volcano views and canyon logistics, while wet-season afternoons are greener but more disruption-prone.
hls: UNESCO historic centre built in volcanic sillar stone
Excellent base for Colca Canyon condor-viewing and trekking
Strong picantería food culture (rocoto relleno, chupe de camarones)
Consistently sunny dry-season skies for photography
High-altitude city break with manageable pace versus Cusco crowds
Good onward links to Puno/Lake Titicaca and southern Peru routes

todo.name: Walk central Arequipa
todo.desc: Use a half-day orientation walk through the historic/core districts and local markets.

todo.name: Museum and heritage stop
todo.desc: Anchor one cultural visit to understand local history and identity.

todo.name: Local food circuit
todo.desc: Plan a focused evening around regional specialties and neighborhood dining.

todo.name: Scenic viewpoint session
todo.desc: Schedule sunrise or sunset from a top city/nearby viewpoint.

todo.name: Nature or coast day trip
todo.desc: Reserve one full day for nearby nature, coast, or mountain access.

todo.name: Flexible slow day
todo.desc: Keep one low-structure day for weather pivots and spontaneous plans.

wifi.note: Urban hotels/cafés are usually stable; speeds can soften in outer districts or remote day-trip zones.

prac.fltNote: No routine UK nonstop service to AQP; most itineraries route London -> Lima (usually nonstop in peak schedules) then a 1h30-1h40 domestic flight to Arequipa. Peru domestic fares spike around Fiestas Patrias (late July) and long weekends, so booking internal legs early reduces risk of expensive last-minute seats.
prac.currency: Peruvian sol (PEN). Cards are common in central districts, but markets, small eateries, and Colca villages often prefer cash.
prac.lang: Spanish is dominant; tourism-facing English is patchy outside central hotels/tour operators.
prac.tz: PET (UTC-5) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Peru grants visa-free short stays to many passports (including UK, US, and EU) but permitted days are set by immigration on entry; check official requirements and onward-ticket expectations before travel.
alerts: Arequipa sits around 2,335 m: keep day one light, hydrate, and avoid hard canyon hikes immediately if you are not acclimatized.
Colca Canyon roads can be slowed by landslides/fog in wet months (roughly Dec-Mar); confirm departure viability with operators the evening before.
In the historic centre, use registered taxis/app rides at night and avoid carrying large cash after picantería/bar closing times.

sweet: April-June and September-October usually give the best balance of clear skies, manageable crowds, and stable canyon transport conditions.
